2606.27736.title: ToE: A Hierarchical and Explainable Claim Verification Framework with Dynamic Multi-source Evidence Retrieval and Aggregation
2606.27736.paper_summary: The rapid spread of fake news poses increasing threats to information ecosystems, especially as AI-generated misinformation under Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) poisoning allows adversarially crafted content to be systematically surfaced by retrieval systems, contaminating LLM reasoning. In this paper, we propose Tree of Evidence (ToE), a hierarchical evidence reasoning framework for automated fact-checking that models each claim as a dynamically expanding argument tree. ToE integrates a reinforcement learning-driven multi-source retrieval agent, an evidence evaluation agent, and an argument tree aggregation algorithm to iteratively decompose, retrieve, and verify claims through an explainable evidence chain. We further provide a theoretical analysis of the retrieval process, deriving a formal error bound that guarantees the learned policy converges to a neighborhood of the information-theoretically optimal policy. Experiments across multiple datasets and backbone LLMs demonstrate that ToE achieves improvements ranging from 4 to 24 percentage points over competitive baselines, with particularly pronounced gains on adversarially poisoned inputs.
2606.27736.paper_summary_zh: 假新聞的快速傳播對信息生態系統構成了日益增長的威脅，尤其是當生成引擎優化（GEO）毒化的AI生成錯誤信息使得對抗性創建的內容能夠被檢索系統系統性地呈現，污染了大型語言模型的推理。在本文中，我們提出了證據樹（ToE），這是一個層次化的證據推理框架，用於自動事實檢查，將每個主張建模為一個動態擴展的論證樹。ToE整合了一個基於強化學習的多源檢索代理、一個證據評估代理和一個論證樹聚合算法，以通過可解釋的證據鏈迭代地分解、檢索和驗證主張。我們進一步提供了檢索過程的理論分析，推導出一個正式的誤差界限，保證所學的策略收斂到信息理論上最佳策略的鄰域。跨多個數據集和主幹大型語言模型的實驗表明，ToE在競爭基準上實現了4到24個百分點的改進，尤其在對抗性毒化的輸入上獲得了特別明顯的增益。
2606.27736.author: Zhaoqi Wang et.al.
2606.27736.authors: Zhaoqi Wang, Zijian Zhang, Kun Zheng, Zhen Li, Xin Li, Chunlei Li, Jiamou Liu
